data.title: Wolftank Secures New, Renews Italian Environmental Contracts
data.description:
Energy and environmental technologies company Wolftank Group secured new and renewed 51.5 million euros worth of environmental contracts in Italy, according to a Thursday release.

The new contracts include a 20 million-euro deal secured by the company's unit Petroltecnica to provide civil works and remove waste at an Italian plant for 24 months, a three-year contract worth 500,000 euros to recover and dispose of petroleum products and chemical substances for Tamoil Raffinazione's depot in Cremona, and a two-year emergency environmental safety management contract from e-Distribuzione valued at 1.2 million euros.

The company also renewed two 30 million euros worth of environmental services, emergency, and engineering services contracts with the Italian oil and energy company Italiana Petroli. Both deals cover a time period of three years.

data.title: BetMakers Looks Simply Too Cheap to Bull — Market Talk
data.description: BetMakers Technology turns Ord Minnett analyst Lindsay Bettiol even more bullish on the basis that its stock is simply too cheap. Bettiol points out in a note to clients that the stock implies an enterprise value for the wagering-tech provider of just 1.0 times revenue. He tells readers that the company's capital raise puts it in an unquestionably strong position, bolstering its balance sheet following a recent return to topline growth. It is also generating positive free cash flow, he adds. Ord Minnett raises its recommendation to buy from speculative buy. Target price lifts by 9.5% to A$0.23. Shares are up 2.4% at A$0.1075. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Freehill Mining Achieves Product Grade of 66% of Iron on Sample From Chile Mine; Shares Rise 20%
data.description:
Freehill Mining achieved a product grade of nearly 66% of iron from material crushed to 6 millimeters after it completed magnetic test work on a 400-kilogram bulk sample of magnetite from the Yerbas Buenas mine in Chile, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The result provides the basis for further magnetic test work to be undertaken in the near term, which will include assessing material crushed to 3 millimeters and 5 millimeters to determine if a higher grade concentrate can be produced, the filing said.

The company will progress mine planning, reviewing different permitting scenarios, and determining the necessary capital expenditure to restart magnetite mining from the historical pit.

The firm's shares rose 20% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Beacon Minerals Plans to Consolidate Issued Capital, Shares Fall 3%
data.description:
Beacon Minerals plans to consolidate the issued capital of the firm at a ratio of 40-to-one, subject to the receipt of shareholder approval at a general meeting to be held on July 7, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm said the consolidation would lead to a more appropriate and effective capital structure and a share price more appealing to a wider range of investors.

Its board will unanimously recommend that the shareholders vote in favor of the consolidation.

It expects to have 105,659,886 shares and 13,343,975 listed options post-consolidation. It currently has 4,226,395,442 shares and 533,759,000 listed options.

Beacon Minerals' shares fell 3% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Huber + Suhner Target Started at CHF100.00 by Berenberg
Huber + Suhner Started at Buy by Berenberg
Australian Equities Roundup — Market Talk
data.description:
0447 GMT - BetMakers Technology turns Ord Minnett analyst Lindsay Bettiol even more bullish on the basis that its stock is simply too cheap. Bettiol points out in a note to clients that the stock implies an enterprise value for the wagering-tech provider of just 1.0 times revenue. He tells readers that the company's capital raise puts it in an unquestionably strong position, bolstering its balance sheet following a recent return to topline growth. It is also generating positive free cash flow, he adds. Ord Minnett raises its recommendation to buy from speculative buy. Target price lifts by 9.5% to A$0.23. Shares are up 2.4% at A$0.1075. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0335 GMT - CAR Group's bull at Citi sees little risk to their 2H forecasts for the Australian vehicle advertiser's U.S. unit. Analyst Siraj Ahmed draws clients' attention to recreational-vehicle maker Thor's 3Q result, which was stronger than market expectations. He writes in a note that this supports his view that RV demand in the U.S. remains steady, which is good news for CAR's Trader Interactive unit. Citi keeps a buy rating and A$42.00 on the stock, which is up 0.4% at A$36.07. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0316 GMT - An improvement in sales of the top 11 models fitted with ARB Corp. equipment is linked almost entirely to a single vehicle, Canaccord Genuity analysts point out. They tell clients in a note that while the 6.3% on-year rise is positive for the Australian accessory manufacturer, it mostly relates to an unusual jump in sales of the Toyota Prado. This stems from strong current sales of a new model comparing with low year-earlier sales as buyers waited on its release, they observe. However, they add that the Prado is a high-fitment rate vehicle, so the surge will be helpful to ARB. Canaccord Genuity has a hold rating and A$34.70 target price on the stock, which is down 0.3% at A$31.77. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0034 GMT - Dundee Precious Metals may not be the only party interested in Adriatic Metals, says Canaccord Genuity. Adriatic recently confirmed it's in talks with Dundee about a potential takeover bid. That led to Adriatic's share price rising some 40%. Canaccord says Dundee has always looked like a potential bidder for Adriatic given similarities between their European assets. "However, we suspect other parties may also be interested in Adriatic's high-grade, low-cost operation," analyst Paul Howard says. Canaccord lifts its price target for Adriatic by 21% to A$5.50/share. That values its equity at US$1.25 billion. Adriatic is unchanged today at A$5.28. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2353 GMT - A visit to Whitehaven Coal's Daunia and Blackwater operations in Australia cements Morgan Stanley's overweight call on its stock. The mines are on track for A$100 million a year in cost savings by the end of FY2025, MS analysts say in a note. They reckon there is also plenty of potential for growth at the sites. However, approvals for the nearby Winchester South development are likely two years away. Morgan Stanley has an A$6.20/share target price on Whitehaven. The stock ended Thursday at A$5.59. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2314 GMT - Morgans pares its earnings view for rural-services provider Elders because it no longer expects the acquisition of Delta Agribusiness will happen in FY 2025. The deal has been held up competition concerns. The Australian Competition & Consumer Commission wants more time to assess the transaction's effects. Morgans analyst Belinda Moore says there's a risk that the regulator will require Elders to sell some stores to secure approval. Morgans had assumed Delta would contribute A$5 million to Elders's Ebit in FY 2025. That has now been removed. "This has resulted in our FY 2025 Ebit and net profit forecasts falling by 6.6% and 8.8% respectively," Morgans says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2302 GMT - Ord Minnett analyst Milo Ferris is shrugging off Cosol's disappointing FY 2025 earnings guidance. The enterprise asset management software company forecast revenue of A$118 million to A$121 million and Ebitda of A$16.3 million to A$16.7 million. At the midpoint, the guidance was 3.3% and 6.4% below Ord Minnett's forecasts, respectively. Still, Ferris says Cosol's momentum entering the fiscal year appears positive. Revenue growth is expected to be in the high-single-digit percentage range as higher value contracts are locked in. "This appears conservative in our view," Ferris says. The bank retains a buy call on Cosol, which ended Thursday at A$0.655/share. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2245 GMT - The four-month delay to the Australian government's Aged Care Act will likely dent the FY 2026 earnings of Regis Healthcare, signals Jefferies. Still, the bank raises its price target by 16%, to A$8.80/share, because other tailwinds are strengthening. "Aged care regulatory reform is increasingly supportive of the sector with a better aligned funding mechanism at the same time as baby boomers have begun entering an under-supplied residential aged care sector," analyst Vanessa Thomson says. Jefferies lowers its FY 2026 Ebitda forecast by 4%, while keeping its FY 2027 outlook unchanged. It retains a "buy" call on Regis Healthcare, which ended Thursday at A$7.61. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Chemring Target Raised to 670p From 470p by Berenberg
Chemring Raised to Buy From Hold by Berenberg
India Cuts Rates by 50bps to 5.50%
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) lowered its key repo rate by 50 basis points to 5.75% at its May meeting, marking the third consecutive monthly rate cut, exceeding market expectations of a 25 basis point reduction.

The latest adjustment brought borrowing costs to their lowest level since August 2022, driven by easing inflation and uncertainty surrounding global trade tensions.

data.title: Fitch Holds Evoke's Rating on Strong Business Profile, Expected Revenue Growth
data.description:
Fitch Ratings on Thursday affirmed its long-term issuer default rating on Evoke at B+, with a negative outlook.

The rating action reflects the gambling company's strong business profile, solid brand portfolio, and expectations for revenue to rise in the mid-single digits between 2025 and 2027. However, the rating remains constrained by the company's weaker profitability in comparison to peers, which continues to weigh on its free cash flow generation.

The negative outlook signals continued pressure from high interest costs forecast until 2026, alongside execution risks to organic deleveraging, Fitch added.

data.title: Golden Mile Resources Finds Visible Copper Carbonate Mineralization in US Project; Shares Down 22%
data.description:
Golden Mile Resources said visible copper carbonate mineralization was intersected in multiple holes across two prospects in its Pearl Copper project in Arizona, US, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Five of the seven holes drilled at the Odyssey Prospect and two of the three holes drilled at the Ford Prospect intersected visual copper mineralisation, the filing added.

The company expects assay results in the coming weeks.

The company's shares plunged 22% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: China's yuan slips after Trump-Xi call leaves issues unresolved
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump and Xi hold call but leave key issues unresolved
  • Investors remain sceptical of a Sino-US trade deal, analyst says
  • Yuan eases as sentiment fails to be lifted, trader says
  • Market attention switches to key economic data releases next week

China's yuan slipped against the dollar on Friday, as U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping held a much-anticipated call but left key issues that have stoked tensions between the world's two largest economies unresolved.

During the more than one-hour-long call, Xi told Trump to back down from trade measures that have roiled the global economy and warned him against threatening steps on Taiwan, according to a Chinese government summary.

"Although the likelihood of a U.S.-China deal increases with more high-level dialogues, investors remain skeptical that both sides are merely buying time to address some pressing issues," said Gary Ng, senior economist at Natixis.

Trump said on social media that the talks focused primarily on trade led to "a very positive conclusion," announcing further lower-level U.S.-China discussions and that "there should no longer be any questions respecting the complexity of Rare Earth products."

"The call does not offer much comfort in cutting tariffs, but only touching on access of critical materials and tech export control," Ng said. "Therefore, there is no certainty of what kind of deal will be made, and it may only be a partial one given the wide range of issues between the U.S. and China."

As of 0400 GMT, the onshore yuan was 0.04% lower at 7.1820 per dollar, while its offshore counterpart was down about 0.06% to 7.1790 at midday.

"The talk itself should lift market sentiment, but Trump's erratic policies make it hard to convince investors," said a trader at a foreign bank.

Wang Zhuo, partner at Zhuozhu Investment in Shanghai, said "Trump's fickleness has made such talks less and less meaningful to the market", but added that direct communications were helpful for removing some misunderstandings.

Trade talks between Washington and Beijing had stalled after a meeting in Geneva last month, where both sides agreed to temporarily roll back most of the tariffs imposed on each other's goods since April. Trump has accused China of violating the bilateral deal.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) set the midpoint rate at 7.1845 per dollar, its strongest since May 26 and 90 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate of 7.1935. The spot yuan is allowed to trade 2% either side of the fixed midpoint each day.

Investors will shift their attention to a string of Chinese economic data releases next week, including inflation and trade data on Monday, as investors are keen to gauge the health of the broader economy under Trump's tariffs.

data.title: Shaanxi Coal Industry's Coal Production, Sales Slip in May
data.description:
Shaanxi Coal Industry's coal production slipped 3% to 14.9 million tons in May from 15.3 million tons in the year-ago period, while sales fell 4.2% to 13.8 million tons from 14.4 million tons a year earlier.

Power generation increased 2.6% to 2.96 billion kilowatt-hours from 2.88 billion kilowatt-hours, while sales jumped 3.6% to 2.77 billion kilowatt-hours from 2.68 billion kilowatt-hours in the prior-year period, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Brigade Enterprises Arm Forms Limited Liability Partnership for Real Estate Development
data.description:
Brigade Tetrarch, a wholly-owned subsidiary of real estate developer Brigade Enterprises , has incorporated a limited liability partnership named Auraterra Developers, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The LLP has been formed with an initial capital contribution of 99,990 rupees, which will be involved real estate project development.

data.title: Indian shares erase losses after RBI delivers a larger-than-expected rate cut
data.description:
Indian shares erased early losses on Friday, after the Reserve Bank of India cut its key repo rate by a larger-than-expected 50 basis points to support economic growth.

The Nifty 50 rose 0.03 to 24,753.45, while the BSE Sensex traded flat at 81,389.48 as of 10:12 a.m. IST.

The indexes were down about 0.2% ahead of the RBI policy decision.

Twelve of the 13 major sectors advanced. The broader, more domestically-focussed smallcaps and midcaps gained about 0.4% each.

data.title: Zhongtong Bus' Sales Slip 4%, Output Drops 20% in May
data.description:
Zhongtong Bus' sales in May slipped 4.2% to 1,001 units from 1,045 units a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen bourse filing on Friday.

The Chinese bus manufacturer's output in the same month dropped 20% to 1,027 buses from 1,277 buses in the previous year.

Between January and May, sales rose 3.5% year on year to 4,813 buses from 4,649 buses, while output fell 7.7% to 4,952 units from 4,952 a year ago, the filing said.

data.title: US Retains Taiwan on Currency Manipulation Watchlist
data.description:
Taiwan remains on the US Treasury's currency manipulation watchlist, according to the department's latest semiannual report on the foreign exchange policies of major trading partners released on Thursday.

The report noted that Taiwan discloses its foreign exchange intervention data only twice a year, with a three-month delay. US officials urged Taiwan to enhance oversight of non-bank financial sector risks, including those linked to foreign exchange, and to minimize intervention, allowing the New Taiwan dollar to adjust in line with market fundamentals.

Taiwan met two of the three criteria used by the US to assess currency manipulation - a large bilateral trade surplus with the US, and a substantial current account surplus - but fell short of meeting the threshold for persistent intervention. The US did not accuse Taiwan of manipulation but maintained it on the watchlist to encourage more transparency and market-driven currency practices.

data.title: Hitachi Energy India Stock Falls 2.34%, Among Top Midcap Losers in Today's Trading Session
data.description:
Hitachi Energy India's stock experienced a decline of 2.34% during today's trading session. The current price stands at Rs 19,058.00, marking a notable downturn for the stock. This movement positions Hitachi Energy India among the top losers in the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index.

Stock Performance vs. NIFTY MIDCAP 150

Hitachi Energy India is a constituent of the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index. The decline in its share price reflects the broader market dynamics and specific pressures faced by the company. Analyzing the stock's performance within this index provides context for investors monitoring mid-cap market trends.

Financial Performance

A review of Hitachi Energy India's financial performance reveals the following:Annual Data:

  • Sales: Sales have increased over the past five years, from Rs 3,420 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 6,384 crore in March 2025.
  • Other Income: Fluctuated, with a high of Rs 66 crore in Mar 2022 and a recent value of Rs 57 crore in March 2025.
  • Total Income: Increased from Rs 3,438 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 6,442 crore in March 2025.
  • Total Expenditure: Rose from Rs 3,282 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 5,880 crore in March 2025.
  • EBIT: Improved from Rs 156 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 561 crore in March 2025.
  • Net Profit: Increased from Rs 99 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 383 crore in March 2025.

Quarterly Data:

  • Sales: Increased from Rs 1,695 crore in March 2024 to Rs 1,883 crore in March 2025.
  • Other Income: Increased from Rs 3 crore in March 2024 to Rs 38 crore in March 2025.
  • Total Income: Increased from Rs 1,699 crore in March 2024 to Rs 1,921 crore in March 2025.
  • Total Expenditure: Increased from Rs 1,535 crore in March 2024 to Rs 1,669 crore in March 2025.
  • EBIT: Increased significantly from Rs 163 crore in March 2024 to Rs 252 crore in March 2025.
  • Net Profit: Increased from Rs 113 crore in March 2024 to Rs 183 crore in March 2025.

Balance Sheet:

  • Share Capital: Remained constant at Rs 8 crore over the past five years.
  • Reserves & Surplus: Increased from Rs 924 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 4,205 crore in March 2025.
  • Total Liabilities: Increased from Rs 3,503 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 8,613 crore in March 2025.
  • Fixed Assets: Increased from Rs 688 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 806 crore in March 2025.
  • Current Assets: Increased from Rs 2,773 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 7,580 crore in March 2025.
  • Total Assets: Increased from Rs 3,503 crore in Dec 2020 to Rs 8,613 crore in March 2025.

Key Ratios:

  • Basic EPS (Rs.): Increased from Rs 23.55 in Dec 2020 to Rs 90.36 in March 2025.
  • Book Value/Share (Rs.): Increased from Rs 219.93 in Dec 2020 to Rs 944.87 in March 2025.
  • Dividend/Share (Rs.): Increased from Rs 0.00 in Dec 2020 to Rs 6.00 in March 2025.

Market Sentiment

According to Moneycontrol analysis, the sentiment for Hitachi Energy India is very bullish as of June 6, 2025.Despite a very bullish sentiment, Hitachi Energy India's share price is currently trading lower at Rs 19,058.00, reflecting a 2.34% decrease. Investors will be closely monitoring the stock's performance in upcoming sessions to gauge future market movements.

data.title: Yuga Labs proposes dismantling ApeCoin DAO over failed governance, replacing it with new entity ApeCo
data.description:
Bored Apes creator Yuga Labs proposed discontinuing its ApeCoin DAO governance system to establish a new operating model for growing the Ethereum-based token Ape's ecosystem.

"ApeCoin DAO was a bold experiment, but one born of a different era," Yuga Labs CEO Greg Solano wrote in the Thursday proposal. "What started with promise has devolved into sluggish, noisy, and often unserious governance theater."

To salvage and advance the DAO's sole remaining merit — funding builders — Yuga Labs proposed replacing it with a new entity, ApeCo. 

The transition aims to eliminate ambiguity in governance and focus resources on building the ecosystem's three core pillars, which are ApeChain, Bored Ape Yacht Club and Otherside. It also plans to empower "real" builders by setting milestone-based grants and tighter accountability.

Founded in 2022, the ApeCoin DAO launched ApeCoin on the Ethereum blockchain as an independent, decentralized entity that is separate from Yuga Labs, despite being connected to its ecosystem.

However, the DAO has since struggled to maintain apt governance operations that benefit the project as a whole. "[The DAO] has been nothing but a joke, and a drag on the entire ecosystem, pillaged and slow and inefficient and overpoliticized since inception, with unaligned bad actors and extractors everywhere," wrote X user @OGDfarmer. "It's clear that [Solano's] Yuga would be better stewards."

If the proposal is accepted, the ApeCoin DAO will be fully terminated, with all rights and powers of token holders related to governance and assets. It would also nullify previous ApeCoin Improvement Proposals (AIPs) and kill delegated authorities, working groups, elections, and forums.

Yuga's latest proposal met with mostly positive feedback in the ApeCoin forum. The proposal has been put to a vote on the forum. It is, however, for checking community sentiment and not an official vote.

Meanwhile, Yuga Labs recently sold off several popular NFT intellectual properties, including Moonbirds, CryptoPunks and Meebits, suggesting that the company is sharpening its focus on its core offerings.

ApeCoin's price has dropped 50% in the past year, currently trading at $0.70, according to The Block's price page. The token is down 97% from its all-time high of $26.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

data.title: Dutch Household Spending Growth at 10-Month Low
data.description:
Household consumption in the Netherlands rose 0.2% year-on-year in April 2025, easing from an upwardly revised 1.2% increase in March.

This marks the softest growth in personal outlays since a decline in June 2024, as goods consumption fell (-0.6% vs 1.5%), driven by reduced purchases of food, beverages, and tobacco (-0.7% vs 2.3%) as well as other goods (-4.9% vs -0.1%).

These declines were partially offset by a rise in durable consumer goods (1.9% vs 1.7%).

Meanwhile, growth in service-related outlays also moderated (0.8% vs 1.1%), though there was higher demand for transport and communication.

Services account for more than half of total household consumption in the country.

data.title: India cenbank delivers larger-than-expected 50 bps cut in key rate
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) cut its key repo rate by a larger-than-expected 50 basis points on Friday, a third consecutive reduction, as muted inflation provided space for policymakers to focus on supporting economic growth.

The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), which consists of three RBI officials and three external members, cut the repo rate (INREPO=ECI) to 5.50%. It has now cut rates by 100 basis points in 2025, starting with a quarter-point reduction in February, its first cut since May 2020. It made a similar-sized cut in April.

Inflation has softened significantly, RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said, adding that there is comfort on inflation aligning with the central bank's target of 4%. Core inflation is also expected to remain benign.

India is already growing at a fast pace but aspires to grow at a higher pace, Malhotra said.

Retail inflation has slowed more quickly than expected in recent months and dropped to a near 6-year low of 3.16% in April, sharply below the RBI's medium-term target of 4%.

India's economy has been resilient, with GDP growth surging to 7.4% in the January-March quarter.

data.title: China to Work with Other Agencies to Ensure Healthy Competition in Auto Market
data.description:
China's Commerce Ministry said it will work with other government agencies to boost compliance guidance to correct "involuntary" competition in the auto market.

The ministry will work to maintain fair competition and promote healthy industry development, Commerce Spokesperson He Yongqian said Thursday.

Commerce officials also met recently with auto industry associations to talk about improvements in auto sales and consumption, He said.

data.title: Amazon to Invest Over $5 Billion in Data Centers in Taiwan
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

Amazon plans to invest over $5 billion in a cluster of data centers in Taiwan. The investment will support the launch of a new cloud services region on the island.

The region will give developers, startups and enterprises more options to run applications and serve end-users from AWS data centers in Taiwan, the company said Thursday.

This will ensure that customers like TSMC and Trend Micro, who want to keep their content onshore, can do so, the Seattle-based tech giant said.

The launch of the AWS Asia Pacific (Taipei) Region shows Amazon's "strong commitment to Taiwan," Cho Jung-tai, the premier of Taiwan, said.

AWS, Amazon's cloud-computing arm, plans to hire and train local staff to operate the new region.

Amazon's multibillion-dollar investment will be used for the construction, connection, operation, and maintenance of its data centers in Taiwan, and signals strong demand for cloud and AI computing.

AWS has more cloud and infrastructure regions in the pipeline, including in Chile, New Zealand and Saudi Arabia.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: DGL Group Says Fiscal 2025 Unaudited Revenue to Be Slightly Higher Year-Over-Year; Shares Hit All-Time Low
data.description:
DGL Group said its unaudited revenue for the second half of fiscal 2025 is projected to be broadly in line with the first half, and the fiscal-year revenue is expected to be slightly higher year-over-year, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) in the second half of fiscal 2025 are expected to be 5% to 10% higher than in the first half. Fiscal 2025 EBITDA is expected to be 15% to 20% lower than fiscal 2024.

The company said its AdBlue business declined as margins normalized following market disruptions in recent years, and due to increased competition. To address these margin pressures, the firm combined its AdBlue production with its crop protection production operations in Victoria and Western Australia.

It plans to relocate out of two warehouses in South Australia and Western Australia over the coming months as a further cost reduction measure. It ceased operating its lead acid battery recycling plant in Victoria due to increased competition raising the cost of acquiring used lead acid batteries.

The company is also undertaking a major systems transition during the year with the installation of new group-wide systems covering ERP and finance, as well as logistics management, the filing said.

DGL Group's shares fell nearly 4% in recent trading on Friday, earlier hitting an all-time low.

data.title: Eastern Polymer Group Target Price Lowered to THB2.50 From THB3.62 by CGS International >EPG.TH
Latest High density polyethylene (HDPE Blow Moulding) offers in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa stated that a Saudi Arabian producer is heard to have offered its HDPE Blow Moulding (B1054) grade at the USD 1070/mt levels.This offer is for June 2025 shipment on CFR Maputo port basis.
